2606.23256.title: P-JEPA: Procedural Video Representation Learning via Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture
2606.23256.paper_summary: The increasing maturity of embodied AI platforms has driven a growing interest in procedural video representation learning to support intelligent assistance systems for complex, multi-step tasks. Leveraging large-scale latent predictive training, video foundation models capture video dynamics, enabling downstream tasks such as activity understanding, spatiotemporal localization, and predictive control. However, procedural videos include actions with long-range dependencies that these models do not support, due to the quadratic complexity of self-attention. Distinct actions, for example, may be visually similar despite appearing at different points in the procedure, such as turning the stove on versus off. Here, we propose a backbone-agnostic approach that learns long-duration video representations by reducing the problem to a dense, frame-aligned action space and predicting pooled masked latent vectors. This approach allows our Procedural Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture (P-JEPA) to ingest videos over 30 minutes long, enabling effective long-form understanding of procedural steps. We evaluate P-JEPA using features extracted with VJEPA2.1, TSM, and I3D over the EgoExo4D, EgoProceL, and Assembly101 datasets, finding that it consistently improves linear separability, streaming inference, and temporal action segmentation performance, achieving state-of-the-art results on EgoExo4D fine-grained action classification while using an order of magnitude fewer parameters than LLM-based methods and running in real time.
2606.23256.paper_summary_zh: 隨著具身人工智慧平台的日益成熟，對程序性視頻表示學習的興趣日益增加，以支持複雜的多步驟任務的智能輔助系統。利用大規模潛在預測訓練，視頻基礎模型捕捉視頻動態，使下游任務如活動理解、時空定位和預測控制得以實現。然而，程序性視頻包含具有長期依賴的動作，而這些模型無法支持，因為自注意力的二次複雜性。不同的動作，例如，可能在程序的不同階段看起來視覺上相似，例如開爐子與關爐子。在這裡，我們提出了一種與骨幹無關的方法，通過將問題簡化為密集的、幀對齊的動作空間並預測聚合的掩蔽潛在向量來學習長時長的視頻表示。這種方法使我們的程序性聯合嵌入預測架構（P-JEPA）能夠處理超過30分鐘長的視頻，有效地理解程序步驟。我們使用從VJEPA2.1、TSM和I3D提取的特徵對P-JEPA進行評估，使用EgoExo4D、EgoProceL和Assembly101數據集，發現它在提高線性可分性、流式推斷和時間動作分割性能方面始終表現良好，在EgoExo4D的細粒度動作分類上達到最先進的結果，同時使用的參數比基於LLM的方法少一個量級，並且能實時運行。
2606.23256.author: Felix Tristram et.al.
2606.23256.authors: Felix Tristram, Stefano Gasperini, Benjamin Killeen, Marcel Walch, Christian Benz, Nassir Navab, Ghazal Ghazaei
